# Checkpoint Lane

A calm triage lane for support engineers working Linux and application checks side by side.

These are prototype sites only. All playbooks below use fake triage data so we can compare structure, scanning speed, and visual style without carrying forward any current ADF content.

## Triage context

- Support engineers are usually working a customer case live over SSH.
- They troubleshoot both the Linux host and the application running on that host.
- Common Linux issues such as disk pressure, inode pressure, OOM kills, file locks, and port conflicts can break the application.
- The first job is triage: find the first broken checkpoint fast and move the case forward with confidence.

## Sample playbooks

<div class="proto-card-grid">
<a class="proto-card" href="/playbooks/application-ui-wont-load/"><strong>Application UI won't load</strong><span>Use this when the customer says the application UI is down and the engineer is already on SSH.</span></a>
<a class="proto-card" href="/playbooks/service-wont-start/"><strong>Application service will not start</strong><span>Use this when a service is stopped, flapping, or exits immediately after restart.</span></a>
<a class="proto-card" href="/playbooks/linux-host-under-pressure/"><strong>Linux host under pressure</strong><span>Use this when multiple services are acting strangely and the host itself may be the real problem.</span></a>
</div>
